spec.name: Always Admit Admission Control Plugin Set
spec.description: The AlwaysAdmit admission controller allows all requests, effectively bypassing other admission controls. It should not be enabled.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins") && arg.contains("AlwaysAdmit"))
)

remediation.manual: Edit the kube-apiserver pod specification file and remove 'AlwaysAdmit' from the --enable-admission-plugins flag.

spec.name: Anonymous Auth Is Not Set To False
spec.description: Anonymous authentication allows unauthenticated users to make requests to the API server. This should be disabled.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && (c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") || c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kubelet")) &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg == "--anonymous-auth=true")) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "--anonymous-auth=true")) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--anonymous-auth=true")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "KubeletConfiguration" && has(object.authentication) && has(object.authentication.anonymous) && object.authentication.anonymous.enabled == true)

remediation.manual: Edit the kube-apiserver pod specification file and set the --anonymous-auth flag to false.

references.title: Controlling Access to the Kubernetes API

spec.name: RBAC Role Allows Impersonation
spec.description: The 'impersonate' verb in RBAC allows a user to act as another user, which can be a significant security risk.
spec.cel: has(object.rules) && object.rules.exists(r, has(r.verbs) && r.verbs.exists(v, v == "impersonate"))

remediation.manual: Review the role and remove the 'impersonate' verb if it is not required.

spec.name: RBAC Role Allows Reading Secrets
spec.description: Roles that allow reading secrets can expose sensitive information and should be tightly controlled.
spec.cel:
has(object.rules) && object.rules.exists(r, 
  has(r.resources) && r.resources.exists(res, res == "secrets") &&
  has(r.verbs) && (r.verbs.exists(v, v == "get") || r.verbs.exists(v, v == "list") || r.verbs.exists(v, v == "watch"))
)

remediation.manual: Review the role and remove permissions to read secrets if not absolutely necessary.

spec.name: RBAC Rule Contains Wildcard
spec.description: RBAC rules should be specific and avoid using wildcards (*) in resources or verbs, as this can grant excessive permissions.
spec.cel:
has(object.rules) && object.rules.exists(r, 
  (has(r.verbs) && r.verbs.exists(v, v == "*")) || 
  (has(r.resources) && r.resources.exists(res, res == "*")) ||
  (has(r.apiGroups) && r.apiGroups.exists(ag, ag == "*"))
)

remediation.manual: Avoid using wildcards in RBAC rules. Instead, explicitly list the required resources and verbs.

spec.name: Service Account Token Automount Not Disabled
spec.description: Automounting service account tokens can be a security risk if the pod does not need to access the Kubernetes API.
spec.cel: !has(object.spec.automountServiceAccountToken) || object.spec.automountServiceAccountToken == true

remediation.manual: If the pod does not require access to the Kubernetes API, set automountServiceAccountToken to false in the Pod or ServiceAccount specification.

spec.name: Image Policy Webhook Admission Control Plugin Not Set
spec.description: The ImagePolicyWebhook admission controller should be enabled to enforce image policies.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("ImagePolicyWebhook"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && cmd.contains("ImagePolicyWebhook")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Enable the `ImagePolicyWebhook` admission plugin in the kube-apiserver configuration.

spec.name: Authorization Mode RBAC Not Set
spec.description: The API server should use RBAC authorization mode to control access to Kubernetes resources.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--authorization-mode=") && arg.contains("RBAC"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--authorization-mode=") && cmd.contains("RBAC")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Set `--authorization-mode=Node,RBAC` for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: Use Service Account Credentials Not Set To True
spec.description: The API server should be configured to use service account credentials for authentication.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-controller-manager") &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg == "--use-service-account-credentials=false")) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "--use-service-account-credentials=false")) ||
    !(
      (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--use-service-account-credentials"))) ||
      (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.startsWith("--use-service-account-credentials")))
    )
  )
)

remediation.manual: Set `--service-account-lookup=true` for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: Role Binding To Default Service Account
spec.description: Binding roles to the `default` service account in a namespace can grant unnecessary permissions to pods that don't explicitly specify a service account.
spec.cel: object.subjects.exists(s, s.kind == "ServiceAccount" && s.name == "default")

remediation.manual: Avoid binding roles to the `default` service account. Create dedicated service accounts with minimal necessary permissions for your applications.

spec.name: Authorization Mode Node Not Set
spec.description: The API server should use `Node` authorization mode to ensure Kubelets can only access resources they are authorized for.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--authorization-mode=") && arg.contains("Node"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--authorization-mode=") && cmd.contains("Node")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Set `--authorization-mode=Node,RBAC` for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: Security Context Deny Admission Control Plugin Not Set
spec.description: The `SecurityContextDeny` admission controller should be enabled to enforce security context constraints.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && (arg.contains("SecurityContextDeny") || arg.contains("PodSecurityPolicy")))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && (cmd.contains("SecurityContextDeny") || cmd.contains("PodSecurityPolicy"))))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Enable the `SecurityContextDeny` admission plugin in the kube-apiserver configuration.

spec.name: Service Account Admission Control Plugin Disabled
spec.description: The `ServiceAccount` admission controller should be enabled to ensure service accounts are properly managed.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--disable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("ServiceAccount"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--disable-admission-plugins=") && cmd.contains("ServiceAccount")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Enable the `ServiceAccount` admission plugin in the kube-apiserver configuration.

spec.name: Dashboard Is Enabled
spec.description: The Kubernetes Dashboard should not be exposed publicly or used in production environments without strong authentication and authorization.
spec.cel: object.kind == "Deployment" && object.metadata.name == "kubernetes-dashboard"

remediation.manual: If the Kubernetes Dashboard is not required, remove its deployment. If it is required, ensure it is not publicly exposed and is secured with strong authentication and authorization.

spec.name: Authorization Mode Set To Always Allow
spec.description: The API server's authorization mode should not be set to `AlwaysAllow`, as this grants unrestricted access to all API requests.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && (c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") || c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kubelet")) &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--authorization-mode=") && arg.contains("AlwaysAllow"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--authorization-mode=") && cmd.contains("AlwaysAllow")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "KubeletConfiguration" && has(object.authorization) && object.authorization.mode == "AlwaysAllow")

remediation.manual: Set `--authorization-mode` to `Node,RBAC` or other restrictive modes for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: Event Rate Limit Admission Control Plugin Not Set
spec.description: The `EventRateLimit` admission controller should be enabled to prevent a single client from overwhelming the API server with events.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("EventRateLimit"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && cmd.contains("EventRateLimit")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Enable the `EventRateLimit` admission plugin in the kube-apiserver configuration.

spec.name: Pod Security Policy Admission Control Plugin Not Set
spec.description: The API server should use `PodSecurityPolicy` plugin to ensure pod security policy work in cluster.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("PodSecurityPolicy"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && cmd.contains("PodSecurityPolicy")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Set `--enable-admission-plugins=PodSecurityPolicy` for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: Service Account Lookup Set To False
spec.description: ServiceAccountLookup must be true
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg == "--service-account-lookup=false")) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "--service-account-lookup=false"))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && object.apiServer.serviceAccountLookup == false)

remediation.manual: Set --service-account-lookup=true

spec.name: Token Auth File Is Set
spec.description: Token auth file must be disabled
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--token-auth-file="))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.startsWith("--token-auth-file=")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && has(object.apiServer.tokenAuthFile))

remediation.manual: Remove --token-auth-file flag

spec.name: Node Restriction Admission Control Plugin Not Set
spec.description: Enable NodeRestriction admission plugin
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("NodeRestriction"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && cmd.contains("NodeRestriction")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && !("NodeRestriction" in object.apiServer.enableAdmissionPlugins))

remediation.manual: Enable NodeRestriction in admission plugins

spec.name: Service Account Key File Not Properly Set
spec.description: Service account key file must be configured
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--service-account-key-file="))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.startsWith("--service-account-key-file=")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && !has(object.apiServer.serviceAccountKeyFile))

remediation.manual: Verify the service account key file exists

spec.name: Namespace Lifecycle Admission Control Plugin Disabled
spec.description: NamespaceLifecycle plugin must be enabled
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--disable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("NamespaceLifecycle"))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.contains("--disable-admission-plugins=") && cmd.contains("NamespaceLifecycle")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && has(object.apiServer.disableAdmissionPlugins) && ("NamespaceLifecycle" in object.apiServer.disableAdmissionPlugins))

remediation.manual: Enable NamespaceLifecycle admission plugin

spec.name: Service Account Name Undefined Or Empty
spec.description: All pods must have a service account
spec.cel: !has(object.spec.serviceAccountName) || object.spec.serviceAccountName == ""

remediation.manual: Define serviceAccountName for all pods

spec.name: RBAC Role Allows Attach
spec.description: RBAC roles must not allow attach
spec.cel: object.kind in ["Role", "ClusterRole"] && object.rules.exists(r, "attach" in r.verbs)

remediation.manual: Remove attach verb from RBAC

spec.name: Service Account Private Key File Not Defined
spec.description: Service account private key must be set
spec.cel:
(object.kind == "Pod" && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-controller-manager") &&
  !(
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--service-account-private-key-file="))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.startsWith("--service-account-private-key-file=")))
  )
)) || (object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && !has(object.apiServer.serviceAccountKeyFile))

remediation.manual: Set --service-account-private-key-file

spec.name: RBAC Role Allows Exec
spec.description: RBAC roles must not allow exec
spec.cel: object.kind in ["Role", "ClusterRole"] && object.rules.exists(r, "exec" in r.verbs)

remediation.manual: Remove exec verbs from RBAC

spec.name: Client Certificate Auth Missing
spec.description: API server must enable client cert auth
spec.cel: object.kind == "ClusterConfiguration" && !has(object.apiServer.clientCAFile)

remediation.manual: Enable --client-ca-file

spec.name: RBAC Roles Allow Privilege Escalation
spec.description: RBAC roles that grant `escalate` permissions can be abused to gain higher privileges.
spec.cel:
object.kind in ["Role", "ClusterRole"] && has(object.rules) && object.rules.exists(r,
  has(r.resources) && has(r.verbs) &&
  r.resources.exists(res, res in ["roles", "clusterroles"]) &&
  r.verbs.exists(verb, verb in ["bind", "escalate", "*"])
)

remediation.manual: Avoid granting `escalate` permissions in RBAC roles.

spec.name: Basic Auth File Is Set
spec.description: Basic authentication is not recommended and should be disabled.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  (
    (has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--basic-auth-file="))) ||
    (has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd.startsWith("--basic-auth-file=")))
  )
)

remediation.manual: Edit the kube-apiserver pod specification file and remove the --basic-auth-file argument.

spec.name: RBAC Roles with Port-Forwarding Permission
spec.description: RBAC roles that grant port-forwarding permissions can be abused to gain access to internal services.
spec.cel: object.kind in ["Role", "ClusterRole"] && object.rules.exists(r, "portforward" in r.verbs)

remediation.manual: Avoid granting `portforward` permissions in RBAC roles unless absolutely necessary.

spec.name: Cluster Admin Rolebinding With Superuser Permissions
spec.description: The `cluster-admin` role should not be bound to regular users or groups, as it provides unrestricted superuser access to the cluster.
spec.cel:
has(object.roleRef) && object.roleRef.kind == "ClusterRole" && object.roleRef.name == "cluster-admin" &&
has(object.subjects) && object.subjects.exists(s, s.kind in ["User", "Group", "ServiceAccount"])

remediation.manual: Avoid binding the `cluster-admin` role to users or groups. Use more specific roles and role bindings to grant only the necessary permissions.

spec.name: Ensure Administrative Boundaries Between Resources
spec.description: Resources should be isolated by namespaces to create administrative boundaries.
spec.cel: object.metadata.namespace == "default"

remediation.manual: Create and use dedicated namespaces for your applications to establish clear administrative boundaries.

spec.name: Shared Service Account
spec.description: Pods should use dedicated service accounts instead of shared or default service accounts.
spec.cel: !has(object.spec.serviceAccountName) || object.spec.serviceAccountName == "default"

remediation.manual: Create a dedicated service account for each application and assign it to the corresponding pods.

spec.name: ServiceAccount Allows Access to Secrets
spec.description: A ServiceAccount that has access to secrets can be a security risk if compromised.
spec.cel:
has(object.subjects) &&
object.subjects.exists(s, s.kind == "ServiceAccount") &&
has(object.roleRef) &&
(object.roleRef.name == "cluster-admin" || object.roleRef.name == "admin" || object.roleRef.name == "edit")

remediation.manual: Avoid binding ServiceAccounts to highly privileged roles like cluster-admin, admin, or edit. Create a dedicated role with the minimum required permissions.

spec.name: Permissive Access to Create Pods
spec.description: Roles should not grant permissions to create pods, as this can be abused to run malicious workloads.
spec.cel:
object.kind in ["Role", "ClusterRole"] &&
has(object.rules) &&
object.rules.exists(r,
  has(r.apiGroups) && r.apiGroups.exists(g, g == "" || g == "*") &&
  has(r.resources) && r.resources.exists(res, res == "pods" || res == "*") &&
  has(r.verbs) && r.verbs.exists(v, v == "create" || v == "*")
)

remediation.manual: Avoid granting 'create' permissions on 'pods' resources. Use more specific roles if pod creation is necessary.

spec.name: Audit Log Path Not Set
spec.description: The audit log path is not set, which means that no audit logs will be generated. Auditing should be enabled to track all activities in the cluster.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  (!has(c.args) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-log-path=")))
)

remediation.manual: Edit the kube-apiserver pod specification file and add the --audit-log-path flag, setting it to a suitable path.

spec.name: Audit Log Maxsize Not Properly Set
spec.description: The maximum size of the audit log file is not set. This can lead to excessive disk usage.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-log-path=")) &&
  !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-log-maxsize="))
)

remediation.manual: Edit the kube-apiserver pod specification file and add the --audit-log-maxsize flag, setting it to a suitable value (e.g., 100).

spec.name: Audit Log Maxage Not Properly Set
spec.description: The maximum age of the audit log files is not set. This can lead to excessive disk usage.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c,
  has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-log-path=")) &&
  !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-log-maxage="))
)

remediation.manual: Edit the kube-apiserver pod specification file and add the --audit-log-maxage flag, setting it to a suitable value (e.g., 30).

spec.name: Audit Policy File Not Defined
spec.description: The API server should be configured with an audit policy file to define what events should be logged.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && (!has(c.args) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-policy-file="))))

remediation.manual: Configure the API server with an audit policy file by setting the `--audit-policy-file` flag.

spec.name: Audit Policy Not Cover Key Security Concerns
spec.description: The audit policy should be configured to log key security events, such as pod creation, secret access, and RBAC changes.
spec.cel:
has(object.spec.containers) &&
object.spec.containers.exists(c, c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") &&
  has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-policy-file="))
)

remediation.manual: Review and update the audit policy file to ensure it logs important security events. This includes pod creation, secret access, and changes to RBAC roles and bindings.

spec.name: Audit Log Maxbackup Not Properly Set
spec.description: The audit log maxbackup should be set to a reasonable value to retain an appropriate number of old log files.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && has(c.args) && !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--audit-log-maxbackup=")))

remediation.manual: Set the `--audit-log-maxbackup` flag for the kube-apiserver to a value of at least 10.

spec.name: Liveness Probe Not Set
spec.description: Liveness probes are essential for application availability. Without them, Kubernetes cannot determine if an application is unresponsive and needs to be restarted.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.livenessProbe))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.livenessProbe)))

remediation.manual: Define a liveness probe for each container in the pod spec.

references.title: Configure Liveness, Readiness and Startup Probes

spec.name: Readiness Probe Not Set
spec.description: Readiness probes are essential for application availability. Without them, Kubernetes may send traffic to a pod that is not yet ready to serve requests.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.readinessProbe))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.readinessProbe)))

remediation.manual: Define a readiness probe for each container in the pod spec.

references.title: Configure Liveness, Readiness and Startup Probes

spec.name: CronJob Deadline Not Configured
spec.description: A starting deadline for a CronJob is important to prevent jobs from running at unexpected times, for example, after a long downtime.
spec.cel: !has(object.spec.startingDeadlineSeconds) || object.spec.startingDeadlineSeconds <= 0

remediation.manual: Set a startingDeadlineSeconds value for the CronJob.

spec.name: StatefulSet Without PodDisruptionBudget
spec.description: StatefulSets should have an associated PodDisruptionBudget to ensure application availability during voluntary disruptions.
spec.cel: !has(object.spec.selector) || !has(object.spec.selector.matchLabels) || size(object.spec.selector.matchLabels) == 0

remediation.manual: Ensure that the StatefulSet has a `spec.selector.matchLabels` defined, and create a PodDisruptionBudget that targets these labels.

spec.name: Deployment Without PodDisruptionBudget
spec.description: Deployments should have an associated PodDisruptionBudget to ensure application availability during voluntary disruptions.
spec.cel: !has(object.spec.selector) || !has(object.spec.selector.matchLabels) || size(object.spec.selector.matchLabels) == 0

remediation.manual: Ensure that the Deployment has a `spec.selector.matchLabels` defined, and create a PodDisruptionBudget that targets these labels.

spec.name: Memory Requests Not Defined
spec.description: Containers should define memory requests to ensure proper scheduling and resource allocation.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory)))

remediation.manual: Set memory requests for all containers in the pod spec.

spec.name: Incorrect Volume Claim Access Mode ReadWriteOnce

spec.name: Terminated Pod Garbage Collector Threshold Not Properly Set
spec.description: The Kube-controller-manager's terminated pod garbage collector threshold should be set to a reasonable value to prevent an accumulation of terminated pods.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-controller-manager") && (!has(c.args) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--terminated-pod-gc-threshold=")) || c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--terminated-pod-gc-threshold=") && arg.contains("12500"))))

spec.name: Deployment Has No PodAntiAffinity

spec.name: StatefulSet Without Service Name

spec.name: CPU Requests Not Set
spec.description: Containers should define CPU requests to ensure proper scheduling and resource allocation.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu)))

remediation.manual: Set CPU requests for all containers in the pod spec.

spec.name: Object Is Using A Deprecated API Version
spec.description: Kubernetes objects should not use deprecated API versions, as they may be removed in future releases, leading to compatibility issues.
spec.cel: object.apiVersion.startsWith("extensions/v1beta1") || object.apiVersion.startsWith("apps/v1beta1") || object.apiVersion.startsWith("apps/v1beta2") || (object.apiVersion.startsWith("policy/v1beta1") && object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy")

spec.name: Using Unrecommended Namespace

spec.name: Metadata Label Is Invalid

spec.name: HPA Targets Invalid Object

spec.cel:

spec.name: HPA Targeted Deployments With Configured Replica Count
spec.description: A Deployment managed by a HorizontalPodAutoscaler (HPA) should not have a static replica count defined in its manifest.

spec.name: Pod or Container Without ResourceQuota

spec.name: CPU Limits Not Set

spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.limits) || !has(c.resources.limits.cpu))) ||

spec.name: Memory Limits Not Defined

remediation.manual: Set memory limits for all containers in the pod spec.

spec.name: StatefulSet Has No PodAntiAffinity
spec.description: StatefulSets should use `podAntiAffinity` to ensure high availability and prevent all replicas from running on the same node.
spec.cel: object.kind == "StatefulSet" && (!has(object.spec.template.spec.affinity) || !has(object.spec.template.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity))

spec.name: Network Policy Is Not Targeting Any Pod

(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.ports) && c.ports.exists(p, has(p.hostIP) && !has(p.hostPort))))

spec.name: Kubelet Event QPS Not Properly Set

spec.name: Insecure Port Not Properly Set
spec.description: The API server's insecure port should be disabled by not setting it or setting it to 0.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--insecure-port=") && !arg.contains("=0")))

remediation.manual: Do not use the --insecure-port flag or set it to 0 for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: TLS Connection Certificate Not Setup
spec.description: The API server should be configured with TLS certificate and key files for secure communication.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && (!has(c.args) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--tls-cert-file=")) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--tls-private-key-file="))))

remediation.manual: Ensure that --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file flags are set for the kube-apiserver.

spec.name: Weak TLS Cipher Suites
spec.description: The API server should be configured to use strong TLS cipher suites.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && has(c.args) && c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--tls-cipher-suites=")))

remediation.manual: Ensure that the `--tls-cipher-suites` flag for the kube-apiserver is set to a list of strong ciphers.

spec.name: Not Unique Certificate Authority
spec.description: The etcd and kubelet certificate authorities should be unique.
spec.cel: true

remediation.manual: Ensure that the certificate authority used for etcd is different from the one used for the kubelet.

spec.name: Secret Exposed as Environment Variable
spec.description: Exposing secrets as environment variables can be risky, as they can be easily exposed through logs or other means.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.env) && c.env.exists(e, has(e.valueFrom) && has(e.valueFrom.secretKeyRef)))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.env) && c.env.exists(e, has(e.valueFrom) && has(e.valueFrom.secretKeyRef))))

remediation.manual: Use a volume to mount the secret into the pod instead of exposing it as an environment variable.

spec.name: Encryption Provider Not Properly Configured
spec.description: The API server should be configured with an encryption provider to encrypt sensitive data at rest in Etcd.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && (!has(c.args) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--encryption-provider-config="))))

remediation.manual: Configure the API server with an encryption provider by setting the `--encryption-provider-config` flag.

spec.name: Encryption Provider Config Is Not Defined
spec.description: The API server should be configured with an encryption provider to encrypt sensitive data at rest in Etcd.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && (!has(c.args) || !c.args.exists(arg, arg.startsWith("--encryption-provider-config="))))

remediation.manual: Configure the API server with an encryption provider by setting the `--encryption-provider-config` flag.

spec.name: Using Kubernetes Native Secret Management
spec.description: Kubernetes secrets should be used for managing sensitive information.
spec.cel: true

remediation.manual: Use Kubernetes secrets to manage sensitive information, such as passwords, OAuth tokens, and ssh keys.

spec.name: Image Without Digest
spec.description: Container images should be referenced by digest rather than by tag to ensure immutability and prevent unexpected image changes.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.image) && !c.image.contains("@sha256:"))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.image) && !c.image.contains("@sha256:")))

remediation.manual: Update image references to use a digest (e.g., `myimage@sha256:digestvalue`) instead of a tag.

spec.name: Always Pull Images Admission Control Plugin Not Set
spec.description: The `AlwaysPullImages` admission controller should be enabled to ensure that images are always pulled from the registry, preventing the use of stale or compromised cached images.
spec.cel: has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.command) && c.command.exists(cmd, cmd == "kube-apiserver") && has(c.args) && !c.args.exists(arg, arg.contains("--enable-admission-plugins=") && arg.contains("AlwaysPullImages")))

remediation.manual: Enable the `AlwaysPullImages` admission plugin in the kube-apiserver configuration.

spec.name: Tiller (Helm v2) Is Deployed
spec.description: Tiller (Helm v2) is deprecated and has known security vulnerabilities. It should not be used.
spec.cel: object.kind == "Deployment" && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, c.image.contains("tiller"))

remediation.manual: Migrate from Helm v2 to Helm v3, which does not use Tiller.

references.title: Helm v2 to v3 Migration Guide

spec.name: Tiller Service Is Not Deleted

spec.cel: object.kind == "Service" && object.metadata.name == "tiller-deploy"

references.title: Helm v2 to v3 Migration Guide

spec.name: Invalid Image Tag
spec.description: Container image tags should be specific and not use mutable tags like 'latest'.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.image) && (c.image.endsWith(":latest") || !c.image.contains(":")))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.image) && (c.image.endsWith(":latest") || !c.image.contains(":"))))

remediation.manual: Use a specific, immutable image tag or a digest to ensure that you are using the correct version of the image.

spec.name: Tiller Deployment Is Accessible From Within The Cluster
spec.description: The Tiller (Helm v2) deployment should not be accessible from within the cluster.
spec.cel: object.kind == "Deployment" && object.metadata.name == "tiller-deploy" && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, c.image.contains("tiller") && (!has(c.ports) || c.ports.exists(p, p.containerPort == 44134)))

remediation.manual: Remove the Tiller deployment or restrict its accessibility from within the cluster.

references.title: Helm v2 to v3 Migration Guide

spec.name: Image Pull Policy Missing
spec.description: ImagePullPolicy must be Always
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.imagePullPolicy) || c.imagePullPolicy != "Always")) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.imagePullPolicy) || c.imagePullPolicy != "Always"))

remediation.manual: Set imagePullPolicy=Always

spec.name: Pod Without Seccomp Profile
spec.description: All pods must define seccompProfile
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.seccompProfile) || c.securityContext.seccompProfile.type != "RuntimeDefault")) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.seccompProfile) || c.securityContext.seccompProfile.type != "RuntimeDefault"))

remediation.manual: Set seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault

spec.name: Container Running With Low UID
spec.description: Containers should not run with a UID less than 10000 to prevent potential privilege escalation.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    (has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000) ||
    ((!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) && 
     (!has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser) || object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000))
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    (has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000) ||
    ((!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) && 
     (!has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser) || object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000))
  ))
)) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    (has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000) ||
    ((!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) && 
     (!has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsUser) || object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000))
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.template.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    (has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000) ||
    ((!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) && 
     (!has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsUser) || object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsUser < 10000))
  ))
))

remediation.manual: Set runAsUser to a value greater than or equal to 10000 in the container's security context.

spec.name: Containers With Added Capabilities
spec.description: Containers should not add Linux capabilities beyond the default set, as this can increase the attack surface.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) && size(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) > 0 &&
    !(has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) && c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(cap, cap == 'ALL') && 
      c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(addCap, addCap == 'NET_BIND_SERVICE'))
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) && size(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) > 0 &&
    !(has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) && c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(cap, cap == 'ALL') && 
      c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(addCap, addCap == 'NET_BIND_SERVICE'))
  ))
)) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) && size(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) > 0 &&
    !(has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) && c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(cap, cap == 'ALL') && 
      c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(addCap, addCap == 'NET_BIND_SERVICE'))
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.template.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) && size(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) > 0 &&
    !(has(c.securityContext.capabilities.drop) && c.securityContext.capabilities.drop.exists(cap, cap == 'ALL') && 
      c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(addCap, addCap == 'NET_BIND_SERVICE'))
  ))
))

remediation.manual: Review and remove any unnecessary added capabilities from the container's security context.

spec.name: No Drop Capabilities for Containers
spec.description: Containers should explicitly drop all capabilities by default and only add back those that are absolutely necessary.

remediation.manual: Add capabilities.drop: ["ALL"] to the container's security context.

spec.name: Missing AppArmor Profile
spec.description: Pods should define an AppArmor profile to restrict container capabilities and enhance security.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && (
  !has(object.metadata.annotations) || 
  (has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    !('container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name in object.metadata.annotations) ||
    object.metadata.annotations['container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name] == 'dummy'
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    !('container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name in object.metadata.annotations) ||
    object.metadata.annotations['container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name] == 'dummy'
  ))
)) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && (
  !has(object.spec.template.metadata.annotations) || 
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    !('container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name in object.spec.template.metadata.annotations) ||
    object.spec.template.metadata.annotations['container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name] == 'dummy'
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.template.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    !('container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name in object.spec.template.metadata.annotations) ||
    object.spec.template.metadata.annotations['container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/' + c.name] == 'dummy'
  ))
))

remediation.manual: Add an AppArmor annotation to the pod or pod template, specifying a profile (e.g., `container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/my-container: runtime/default`).

spec.name: PSP Set To Privileged
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should not allow privileged containers, as this grants extensive root capabilities to the container.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.privileged) && object.spec.privileged == true

remediation.manual: Set `privileged: false` in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: Container Is Privileged
spec.description: Containers should not run in privileged mode, as this grants all capabilities to the container and removes all security restrictions.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.privileged) && c.securityContext.privileged == true)) ||
  (has(object.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.initContainers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.privileged) && c.securityContext.privileged == true))
)) || (object.kind != 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.privileged) && c.securityContext.privileged == true)) ||
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.template.spec.initContainers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.privileged) && c.securityContext.privileged == true))
))

remediation.manual: Set `privileged: false` in the container's security context.

spec.name: Container Runs Unmasked
spec.description: Containers should not run with the `/proc` filesystem unmasked, as this can expose sensitive host information.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.allowedProcMountTypes) && object.spec.allowedProcMountTypes.exists(pmt, pmt == "Unmasked")

remediation.manual: Set `procMount: Default` or omit the `procMount` field in the container's security context.

spec.name: NET_RAW Capabilities Disabled for PSP
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should disallow the `NET_RAW` capability, which allows a container to perform raw socket operations, potentially leading to network attacks.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.requiredDropCapabilities) && !object.spec.requiredDropCapabilities.exists(cap, cap == "NET_RAW") && !object.spec.requiredDropCapabilities.exists(cap, cap == "ALL")

remediation.manual: Add `NET_RAW` to the `requiredDropCapabilities` list in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: Workload Mounting With Sensitive OS Directory
spec.description: Workloads should not mount sensitive host directories (e.g., `/etc`, `/var`, `/usr`) with write permissions, as this can lead to privilege escalation or system compromise.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'PersistentVolume' && has(object.spec.hostPath) && (
  object.spec.hostPath.path == '/' || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/etc') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/var') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/usr') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/proc') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/sys') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/root') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/dev') || 
  object.spec.hostPath.path.startsWith('/boot')
)) ||
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath) && (
  v.hostPath.path == '/' || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/etc') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/var') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/usr') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/proc') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/sys') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/root') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/dev') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/boot')
))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && object.kind != 'PersistentVolume' && has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) && object.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath) && (
  v.hostPath.path == '/' || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/etc') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/var') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/usr') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/proc') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/sys') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/root') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/dev') || 
  v.hostPath.path.startsWith('/boot')
)))

remediation.manual: Avoid mounting sensitive host directories. If necessary, ensure they are mounted as read-only by setting `readOnly: true` in the volume mount.

spec.name: PSP Allows Sharing Host IPC
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should not allow sharing the host IPC namespace, as this can expose sensitive inter-process communication.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.hostIPC) && object.spec.hostIPC == true

remediation.manual: Set `hostIPC: false` in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: PSP Allows Containers To Share The Host Network Namespace
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should not allow containers to share the host network namespace, as this can expose the host's network interfaces.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.hostNetwork) && object.spec.hostNetwork == true

remediation.manual: Set `hostNetwork: false` in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: Docker Daemon Socket is Exposed to Containers
spec.description: Exposing the Docker daemon socket (`/var/run/docker.sock`) to containers can lead to container escape and host compromise.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath) && v.hostPath.path == '/var/run/docker.sock')) ||
(object.kind == 'CronJob' && has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes) && object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath) && v.hostPath.path == '/var/run/docker.sock')) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && object.kind != 'CronJob' && has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) && object.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath) && v.hostPath.path == '/var/run/docker.sock'))

remediation.manual: Do not mount the Docker daemon socket into containers.

spec.name: Root Container Not Mounted Read-only
spec.description: Containers running as root should have their root filesystem mounted as read-only to prevent unauthorized writes.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    ((has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser == 0) ||
     (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) ||
     (!has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser))) &&
    (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) || c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == false)
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    ((has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser == 0) ||
     (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) ||
     (!has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser))) &&
    (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) || c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == false)
  ))
)) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    ((has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser == 0) ||
     (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) ||
     (!has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsUser))) &&
    (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) || c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == false)
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.template.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    ((has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser == 0) ||
     (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.runAsUser)) ||
     (!has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.template.spec.securityContext.runAsUser))) &&
    (!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) || c.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == false)
  ))
))

remediation.manual: Set `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true` for containers running as root.

spec.name: PSP With Added Capabilities
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should not allow adding capabilities beyond the default set, as this can increase the attack surface.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.allowedCapabilities) && size(object.spec.allowedCapabilities) > 0

remediation.manual: Remove unnecessary capabilities from `allowedCapabilities` in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: PSP Allows Sharing Host PID
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should not allow sharing the host PID namespace, as this can expose host processes to containers.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.hostPID) && object.spec.hostPID == true

remediation.manual: Set `hostPID: false` in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: PSP Not Limited Capabilities
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should explicitly define allowed and dropped capabilities to limit the privileges of containers.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && (!has(object.spec.allowedCapabilities) || !has(object.spec.requiredDropCapabilities)) && !(has(object.spec.requiredDropCapabilities) && object.spec.requiredDropCapabilities.exists(cap, cap == "ALL"))

remediation.manual: Define `allowedCapabilities` and `requiredDropCapabilities` in the PodSecurityPolicy to explicitly control container capabilities.

spec.name: PSP With Unrestricted Access to Host Path
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should restrict access to host paths to prevent containers from accessing sensitive host filesystems.
spec.cel:
object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && (
  (!has(object.spec.allowedHostPaths) || size(object.spec.allowedHostPaths) == 0) ||
  (has(object.spec.allowedHostPaths) && object.spec.allowedHostPaths.exists(ahp, 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/dev') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/proc') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/sys') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/etc') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/var') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/usr') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/root') || 
    ahp.pathPrefix.startsWith('/boot') ||

remediation.manual: Restrict `hostPath` volumes in the PodSecurityPolicy by specifying `allowedHostPaths` with `pathPrefix` and ensuring `readOnly: true` for sensitive mounts.

spec.name: Containers With Sys Admin Capabilities
spec.description: Containers should not be granted the `SYS_ADMIN` capability, as it provides a wide range of powerful permissions.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) && c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap, cap == "SYS_ADMIN"))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities) && has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) && c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap, cap == "SYS_ADMIN")))

remediation.manual: Remove the `SYS_ADMIN` capability from the container's security context.

spec.name: PSP Allows Privilege Escalation
spec.description: Pod Security Policies (PSPs) should not allow privilege escalation.
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && (!has(object.spec.allowPrivilegeEscalation) || object.spec.allowPrivilegeEscalation == true)

remediation.manual: Set `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false` in the PodSecurityPolicy.

spec.name: Volume Mount With OS Directory Write Permissions
spec.description: HostPath volumes mounting sensitive OS directories should be read-only.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    has(c.volumeMounts) && c.volumeMounts.exists(vm, 
      (vm.mountPath.startsWith('/etc') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/var') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/usr') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/bin')) &&
      ((!has(vm.readOnly) || vm.readOnly == false) || (has(vm.recursiveReadOnly) && vm.recursiveReadOnly == 'Disabled')) &&
      has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, v.name == vm.name && 
        (has(v.hostPath) || has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.scaleIO) || (!has(v.configMap) && !has(v.secret))))
    )
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    has(c.volumeMounts) && c.volumeMounts.exists(vm, 
      (vm.mountPath.startsWith('/etc') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/var') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/usr') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/bin')) &&
      ((!has(vm.readOnly) || vm.readOnly == false) || (has(vm.recursiveReadOnly) && vm.recursiveReadOnly == 'Disabled')) &&
      has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, v.name == vm.name && 
        (has(v.hostPath) || has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.scaleIO) || (!has(v.configMap) && !has(v.secret))))
    )
  ))
)) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && (
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, 
    has(c.volumeMounts) && c.volumeMounts.exists(vm, 
      (vm.mountPath.startsWith('/etc') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/var') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/usr') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/bin')) &&
      ((!has(vm.readOnly) || vm.readOnly == false) || (has(vm.recursiveReadOnly) && vm.recursiveReadOnly == 'Disabled')) &&
      has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) && object.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, v.name == vm.name && 
        (has(v.hostPath) || has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.scaleIO) || (!has(v.configMap) && !has(v.secret))))
    )
  )) ||
  (has(object.spec.template.spec.initContainers) && object.spec.template.spec.initContainers.exists(c, 
    has(c.volumeMounts) && c.volumeMounts.exists(vm, 
      (vm.mountPath.startsWith('/etc') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/var') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/usr') || vm.mountPath.startsWith('/bin')) &&
      ((!has(vm.readOnly) || vm.readOnly == false) || (has(vm.recursiveReadOnly) && vm.recursiveReadOnly == 'Disabled')) &&
      has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) && object.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, v.name == vm.name && 
        (has(v.hostPath) || has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.scaleIO) || (!has(v.configMap) && !has(v.secret))))
    )
  ))
))

remediation.manual: Set `readOnly: true` for hostPath volume mounts that access sensitive OS directories.

spec.name: Non Kube System Pod With Host Mount
spec.description: Pods outside of the `kube-system` namespace should not use hostPath volumes.
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'PersistentVolume' && has(object.spec.hostPath)) ||
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))) ||
(object.kind == 'Revision' && has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && object.kind != 'PersistentVolume' && object.kind != 'Revision' && has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) && object.spec.template.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath)))

remediation.manual: Avoid using hostPath volumes for pods outside of the `kube-system` namespace.

spec.name: NET_RAW Capabilities Not Being Dropped
spec.description: NET_RAW capability must be dropped

remediation.manual: Drop NET_RAW capability in securityContext

spec.name: Privilege Escalation Allowed
spec.description: Containers must not run with allowPrivilegeEscalation=true

remediation.manual: Set allowPrivilegeEscalation=false in securityContext

spec.name: Root Containers Admitted
spec.description: Containers must not run as UID 0
spec.cel: object.kind == "PodSecurityPolicy" && has(object.spec.runAsUser) && object.spec.runAsUser.rule == "RunAsAny"

remediation.manual: Set runAsNonRoot=true and runAsUser>=1000

spec.name: Container Running As Root
spec.description: Containers must not run as UID 0
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && ((has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser == 0) || (!has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && (!has(c.securityContext.runAsNonRoot) || !c.securityContext.runAsNonRoot))))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, has(c.securityContext) && ((has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && c.securityContext.runAsUser == 0) || (!has(c.securityContext.runAsUser) && (!has(c.securityContext.runAsNonRoot) || !c.securityContext.runAsNonRoot)))))

remediation.manual: Set runAsNonRoot=true and runAsUser>=1000

spec.name: Pod Without Security Context
spec.description: All pods must define securityContext
spec.cel:
(object.kind == 'Pod' && has(object.spec.containers) && object.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.securityContext))) ||
(object.kind != 'Pod' && has(object.spec.template.spec.containers) && object.spec.template.spec.containers.exists(c, !has(c.securityContext)))

remediation.manual: Define securityContext for all pods
